to reach some agreement for COM
plete new briggs in all parts OF the
city wherever needed.


The offers of the streetcar COM
panies to stand half the expense
of the new bridges over streets
traversed by their lines was made
to the board some time ago.


The board wants the council to
take some official action in accep-
lance of these offers to order the
board to go ahead with immediate
construction Of these bridges and
to select a style Of bridge, pre-
ferable reinforced concrete.


The council will also be asked to
have the streetcar companies make
written agreements to stand half
the expense and also to tie up the
Washington Water Power company
in writing regarding the resolution
of the council that the company
stand all the expense Of the tenn
porary structure over the collapsed
span on Howard street.
